questions.question: Which of the following is the most significant consequence of DNA copying during reproduction?

options.A: It ensures the parent remains alive
options.B: It leads to the exact elimination of all variations
options.C: It creates variations that are useful for the survival of the species over time
options.D: It prevents the formation of new cells

questions.explanation: DNA copying is not absolutely accurate, and the resulting variations are the basis for evolution and survival of species in changing environments.

questions.question: In 'Leishmania', binary fission occurs in a definite orientation because of:

options.B: The presence of a whip-like structure (flagellum) at one end
options.C: The circular shape of the cell
options.D: The lack of a cell wall

questions.explanation: Unlike Amoeba, Leishmania has a flagellum at one end, so binary fission occurs in a specific longitudinal plane in relation to this structure.

questions.question: Multiple fission is a method of reproduction observed in:
questions.explanation: Plasmodium (the malarial parasite) divides into many daughter cells simultaneously by multiple fission.

questions.question: Fragmentation is observed in organisms with simple body organization like Spirogyra, but not in complex multicellular organisms because:

options.A: Complex organisms have a very small number of cells
options.B: In complex organisms, specialized cells are organized into tissues and organs
options.C: Complex organisms do not have DNA

questions.explanation: In complex organisms, cell-by-cell division is impractical because cells are organized into tissues and organs placed at specific locations.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Regeneration'?

options.A: It is the same as reproduction for all organisms
options.B: It is carried out by specialized regenerative cells
options.C: It is a deliberate method of reproduction in complex animals
options.D: It occurs only in unicellular organisms

questions.explanation: Regeneration involves specialized cells that proliferate and make large numbers of cells, but it is not the same as reproduction because most organisms would not normally depend on being cut up to reproduce.

questions.question: Vegetative propagation is practiced to grow certain plants because:

options.A: It produces plants that are genetically different from the parent
options.B: Plants raised this way can bear flowers and fruits earlier than those from seeds
options.C: It is the only way for all plants to reproduce

questions.explanation: Plants raised by vegetative propagation (like grafting or layering) can produce flowers and fruits much earlier than those raised from seeds.

questions.question: In a flower, 'Self-pollination' refers to the transfer of pollen from:

options.A: Anther of one flower to stigma of another flower on a different plant
options.B: Anther to stigma of the same flower
options.C: Stigma to anther of the same flower

questions.explanation: If the transfer of pollen occurs in the same flower or another flower on the same plant, it is referred to as self-pollination.

questions.question: The 'Pollen tube' develops after pollination to:

options.A: Protect the pollen grain from drying
options.B: Carry the male germ-cell to the female germ-cell in the ovary
options.D: Help the flower turn into a leaf

questions.explanation: The pollen tube grows out of the pollen grain and travels through the style to reach the ovules in the ovary.

questions.question: Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of two germ cells. To maintain the constant chromosome number in the offspring, the germ cells must:

options.A: Have double the number of chromosomes
options.B: Have half the number of chromosomes compared to non-reproductive cells

questions.explanation: Germ cells are produced by a special type of cell division (meiosis) that halves the chromosome number, so fusion restores the original number in the zygote.

questions.question: The testes in human males are located outside the abdominal cavity in the scrotum because:

options.A: They are too large to fit inside
options.B: Sperm formation requires a lower temperature than the normal body temperature
options.C: It helps in the easy transport of urine
options.D: They need more oxygen from the air

questions.explanation: Sperm formation requires a temperature about 2-3°C lower than the core body temperature ($37^\circ C$).

questions.question: What is the function of the secretions from the prostate gland and seminal vesicles?

options.B: To make the transport of sperm easier and provide nutrition
options.C: To regulate the temperature of the testes

questions.explanation: These glands add their secretions so that the sperm are in a fluid which makes transport easier and provides energy/nutrition.

questions.question: If the egg is not fertilized in a human female, what happens to the lining of the uterus?

options.A: It stays thick to wait for the next month
options.C: It slowly breaks and comes out through the vagina as blood and mucus
options.D: It is absorbed by the ovaries

questions.explanation: This process is called menstruation, which lasts for about two to eight days.

questions.question: Which of the following birth control methods can also provide protection against sexually transmitted diseases (STDs)?
questions.explanation: Condoms act as a mechanical barrier that prevents the transmission of many STDs like HIV-AIDS and Gonorrhoea.

questions.question: A bisexual flower contains:
questions.explanation: Flowers like Hibiscus and mustard are bisexual as they contain both male and female reproductive parts.

questions.question: In 'Rhizopus' (bread mould), the reproductive structures are found in:
questions.explanation: The sporangia contain spores that can eventually develop into new Rhizopus individuals.

questions.question: The process where the embryo is formed from the zygote and begins to develop into a seedling under appropriate conditions is called:
questions.explanation: Germination is the growth of a seed into a seedling.

questions.question: The common passage for both sperm and urine in human males is the:
questions.explanation: The urethra forms a common passage for both sperm and urine, though not at the same time.

questions.question: Surgical blocking of the fallopian tubes to prevent pregnancy is known as:
questions.explanation: In females, the fallopian tubes are blocked to prevent the egg from reaching the uterus.

questions.question: The 'Placenta' contains villi on the:
questions.explanation: The placenta is a disc-like tissue embedded in the uterine wall. It contains villi on the embryo’s side of the tissue to increase surface area.

questions.question: Unisexual flowers like papaya and watermelon require which process for reproduction?
questions.explanation: Since they contain only one sex organ per flower, pollen must be transferred from a different flower (cross-pollination).

questions.question: During puberty, which change is common to both boys and girls?

options.C: Thick hair growth in armpits and genital area

questions.explanation: While many changes are sex-specific, hair growth in these areas occurs in both genders.

questions.question: Oral contraceptive pills prevent pregnancy by:

options.C: Changing the hormonal balance so that eggs are not released

questions.explanation: Oral pills act by changing the body's hormonal balance to inhibit ovulation.

questions.question: The two main functions of the 'Ovaries' are:
questions.explanation: Ovaries are responsible for producing the female germ cells (eggs) and hormones like estrogen.

questions.question: Identical twins are formed when:

options.A: Two eggs are fertilized by two sperms
options.B: One egg is fertilized by two sperms
options.C: One fertilized egg splits into two separate embryos
options.D: Two eggs are fertilized by one sperm

questions.explanation: While not detailed deeply in NCERT, it follows the logic of the zygote dividing into two separate individuals.

questions.question: Tissue culture is a method of:
questions.explanation: Tissue culture involves growing new plants from a small piece of tissue or cells from the growing tip of a parent plant in an artificial medium.
